root_cause_proof.issue: execution semantics were expressed through repeated raw BUY_YES/BUY_NO branching instead of one explicit held-contract price concept, leaving TP/SL and close monitoring audit-ambiguous after the dashboard held-price fix

fix_point.risk: added _held_bid(side, ob) and replaced raw mark branching with current_held_price / held_unrealized_pct naming
fix_point.paper_exec: added _held_bid(side, ob), normalized mark_to_market_value to explicit current_held_price + held_exit_sim, and clarified close_position with held-side exit naming

before.risk_check_exit: if BUY_YES: mark = ob.bid_yes else: mark = ob.bid_no; unrealized_pct = (mark - entry_px) / entry_px
before.risk_mark_to_market: if BUY_YES: mark = ob.bid_yes else: mark = ob.bid_no; return (mark - entry_px) * qty

after.paper_exec_mark_to_market_value: current_held_price = _held_bid(trade.side, ob); held_exit_sim = current_held_price * (1.0 - PAPER_SLIPPAGE_PCT); return (held_exit_sim - entry_px) * qty

proof_summary: Execution monitoring and exit pricing are now explicitly normalized around current_held_price. SEA/TEX-style BUY_NO gains stay positive through monitoring, and BOS/MIL-style opposite-side close ambiguity is removed from the changed execution paths.
